description_long: This large warehouse features a pair of expansive, gently sloped roofs with prominent central ridges, flanked by flat-roofed annexes on either side. The main structure is rectangular, with a grid of skylights or translucent panels visible on the roof, allowing natural light into the interior. The exterior is primarily clad in light gray metal panels, with green landscaping and paved areas surrounding the building. Multiple loading bays and access points are distributed along the longer sides, suggesting high logistical capacity. The site includes driveways, parking areas, and what appears to be a small office or security building at one corner. The overall design is functional, emphasizing efficient movement of goods and vehicles.
description: A sprawling, light gray metal warehouse with dual sloped roofs, skylights, and multiple loading bays, surrounded by paved and landscaped areas.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the twin sloped roofs, central ridges, and the main entry area with adjacent landscaping and driveways.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the building's length, flat-roofed annexes, and the arrangement of loading bays along the facade.
description_view_2: The rear angled view shows the full extent of the sloped roofs, skylight grid, and additional access points or service areas.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals the long, flat annexes, paved service roads, and a small auxiliary structure at the corner.
attribute_facade_design: The facade is dominated by large, light gray metal panels with minimal ornamentation, punctuated by a series of loading bays and entry doors. There are few windows, and the exterior is complemented by paved driveways and landscaped green strips.
attribute_roof_structure: The roof consists of two large, gently sloped sections with central ridges and a grid of skylights or translucent panels. The material appears to be metal, and the roof is free of visible HVAC units or other large equipment.
